tasks.description: Domain: add libs/domain/src/task_contract.rs with TaskContractDocument, ContractedEntryRef, PreReviewGateViolation, and PreReviewGateOutcome as specified in domain-types.json. Implement inherent impls (TaskContractDocument::new / schema_version / track_id / entries; ContractedEntryRef::new / layer / entry_key; PreReviewGateOutcome::blocked) and derive Debug + Clone + PartialEq + Eq for all four types. Wire the new module into libs/domain/src/lib.rs as `pub mod task_contract`. Also update .harness/capabilities/impl-planner.md to extend Phase 3 output responsibility: add task-contract.json as a third written artifact alongside impl-plan.json and task-coverage.json (IN-02, AC-01).

Usecase: add libs/usecase/src/pre_review_gate.rs with PreReviewGateCommand, PreReviewGateError, PreReviewGateInteractor, PreReviewGateService (primary application service trait), TaskContractReaderPort (secondary port), and ImplCatalogSignalReaderPort (secondary port) as specified in usecase-types.json. Implement PreReviewGateInteractor::new(task_contract_reader, signal_reader) and impl PreReviewGateService for PreReviewGateInteractor. PreReviewGateCommand carries track_id: TrackId and layer: Option<LayerId>; Some(layer) checks one TDDD layer and None visits the 6 canonical TDDD layer ids. Gate logic executes three phases for each selected layer with a readable TypeSignalsDocument; all-layers mode uses ImplCatalogSignalReaderPort::read_optional_signals so an absent signal document is treated as no entries to verify, while read/decode failures still return SignalReadFailed. Per-layer mode uses ImplCatalogSignalReaderPort::read_signals and therefore fails closed when the requested layer document is absent. For each loaded signal document: (1) Scope discovery and attribution completeness derives the full set of scope-relevant catalogue entry keys from that signal document, then flattens task-contract.json entries where entry.layer == selected layer into the attributed set; any scope-relevant signal entry absent from the attributed set is an OrphanEntry violation; (2) Referential integrity verifies every attributed entry for the selected layer exists in that TypeSignalsDocument; non-existent entries produce InvalidEntryRef violations; (3) Signal check requires all attributed scope-relevant entries to have impl_catalog signal Blue; Yellow or Red entries produce NonBlueSignal violations. On Passed, conformance_summary text follows D5 wording discipline ('宣言した API surface が型契約と shape 一致（body は未検証 — stub / liveness は reviewer が確認）'). Derive / trait impls per catalogue: Debug + Clone + PartialEq + Eq for Command; Debug + Display + Error for Error; PreReviewGateService for Interactor. Wire into libs/usecase/src/lib.rs.

Infrastructure: add three modules to libs/infrastructure/src/. (1) task_contract_codec: implement TaskContractDocumentDto (serde-serializable mirror of domain::task_contract::TaskContractDocument with schema_version / track_id / entries fields; derive Serialize + Deserialize + Debug) and TaskContractCodecError (Json(serde_json::Error) / UnsupportedSchemaVersion { found, expected } / Validation(String) variants; derive Debug + Display + Error + From<serde_json::Error>); add free functions `decode(&[u8]) -> Result<TaskContractDocument, TaskContractCodecError>` (validates schema_version before constructing the domain object) and `encode(&TaskContractDocument) -> Result<Vec<u8>, TaskContractCodecError>`. (2) task_contract_reader: implement FsTaskContractReader (items_dir: PathBuf injected via new(items_dir: PathBuf) -> Self) implementing usecase::pre_review_gate::TaskContractReaderPort; reads <items_dir>/<track_id>/task-contract.json via decode; maps missing file to PreReviewGateError::TaskContractNotFound, I/O and codec errors to TaskContractReadFailed. (3) impl_catalog_signal_reader: implement FsImplCatalogSignalReader (items_dir: PathBuf) implementing usecase::pre_review_gate::ImplCatalogSignalReaderPort; read_signals reads <items_dir>/<track_id>/<layer>-type-signals.json as domain::TypeSignalsDocument and maps absence/read/decode errors to SignalReadFailed; read_optional_signals returns Ok(None) only for positively classified absence and maps other errors to SignalReadFailed. Wire all three modules into libs/infrastructure/src/lib.rs.

CLI driver: add apps/cli-driver/src/task_contract.rs with TaskContractInput (enum with Check { layer: Option<String>, track_id: String }) and TaskContractDriver (struct holding Arc<dyn usecase::pre_review_gate::PreReviewGateService>). Implement TaskContractDriver::new(service: Arc<dyn PreReviewGateService>) -> Self and TaskContractDriver::handle(input: TaskContractInput) -> CommandOutcome: for the Check variant, parse track_id into TrackId and parse Some(layer) into LayerId while preserving None for all-layers mode, then call PreReviewGateService::check(PreReviewGateCommand { track_id, layer }); on Passed emit conformance_summary to stdout and return CommandOutcome::Ok; on Blocked emit violation list to stderr and return CommandOutcome::Err(1); propagate PreReviewGateError variants as CommandOutcome::Err. items_dir is intentionally not part of TaskContractInput: the composition root injects filesystem adapters built from the requested items_dir before constructing the driver. Derive Debug + Clone for TaskContractInput; Debug for TaskContractDriver. Wire the module into apps/cli-driver/src/lib.rs.

CLI: add apps/cli/src/commands/task_contract.rs with TaskContractCommand (enum with Check(TaskContractCheckArgs) annotated with clap Subcommand derive), TaskContractCheckArgs (struct with layer: Option<String>, track_id: String, items_dir: PathBuf defaulting to 'track/items', annotated with clap Args derive), and free functions `execute(cmd: TaskContractCommand) -> ExitCode` (dispatch to execute_* handler) and `execute_task_contract_check(args: TaskContractCheckArgs) -> ExitCode` (constructs TaskContractCompositionRoot, calls task_contract_check(layer, track_id, items_dir), converts CommandOutcome to ExitCode: 0 on Passed, 1 on Blocked or error). The `--layer` flag is optional; omitting it enters all-layers mode, which visits the 6 canonical TDDD layer ids and combines outcomes for readable signal documents while treating absent signal documents as no entries to verify. Derive Debug + Clone for TaskContractCommand and TaskContractCheckArgs. Register the `task-contract` top-level subcommand in the main CLI router (apps/cli/src/main.rs or the top-level clap Subcommand enum). Wire the module into apps/cli/src/commands/mod.rs.

CLI composition: add apps/cli-composition/src/task_contract.rs with TaskContractCompositionRoot (unit struct). Implement new() -> Self, task_contract_driver(&self, items_dir: PathBuf) -> cli_driver::task_contract::TaskContractDriver (constructs FsTaskContractReader and FsImplCatalogSignalReader from the requested items_dir, builds PreReviewGateInteractor, wraps as Arc<dyn PreReviewGateService>, returns TaskContractDriver::new(service)), and task_contract_check(&self, layer: Option<String>, track_id: String, items_dir: PathBuf) -> Result<cli_driver::CommandOutcome, CompositionError> (builds a driver via task_contract_driver(items_dir), then dispatches TaskContractInput::Check { layer, track_id }; the driver remains the single primary adapter responsible for parsing CLI strings, invoking the use case, and rendering Passed/Blocked outcomes). Impl Default. Wire the module into apps/cli-composition/src/lib.rs.

Unit tests: add #[cfg(test)] unit tests for PreReviewGateInteractor covering all AC-07 cases. Use in-module mock struct implementations of TaskContractReaderPort and ImplCatalogSignalReaderPort (or rstest fixtures). Required cases: (a) TaskContractReaderPort returns TaskContractNotFound error → outcome is Blocked with MissingTaskContract violation; (b) TypeSignalsDocument contains a scope-relevant entry whose layer matches the selected layer, but task-contract.json attributes no task to that entry → outcome is Blocked with OrphanEntry violation identifying the missing entry; (c) attributed entry's key does not exist in the TypeSignalsDocument returned by ImplCatalogSignalReaderPort (referential integrity violation) → outcome is Blocked with InvalidEntryRef violation; (d) attributed entry exists in TypeSignalsDocument but has impl_catalog signal Yellow or Red → outcome is Blocked with NonBlueSignal violation carrying the entry and signal; (e) all attributed scope-relevant entries have Blue signal and attribution is complete → outcome is Passed with conformance_summary that contains '型契約と shape 一致' (or the D5-compliant text authored in T002) and '未検証'. Confirm all five cases pass. No integration tests required in this task; AC-07 is satisfied by the unit test coverage here plus cargo make ci in the final batch commit.

Makefile.toml wiring: modify [tasks.track-local-review-fix-codex] to prepend the task-contract gate before `bin/sotp review fix-local`. The revised thin-chain script must: (1) strip a leading `--` separator when present and extract --track-id from the forwarded $@ arguments; (2) run `bin/sotp task-contract check --track-id "$_TRACK_ID" --items-dir track/items` without --layer so PreReviewGateCommand.layer is None and all-layers mode visits the 6 canonical TDDD layer ids, combines outcomes for readable signal documents, and treats absent signal documents as no entries to verify; (3) resolve CODEX_BIN and invoke `bin/sotp review fix-local "$@"` only after the gate exits 0; (4) propagate non-zero exit from task-contract check so review fix-local is not reached when the all-layers gate blocks on violations or signal read/decode errors. No sibling track-local-review-fix-claude task exists in Makefile.toml; only track-local-review-fix-codex requires modification. T008 is committed as a separate batch from T001-T007.

sections.title: Foundation: domain model and usecase gate contracts
description: T001 lays down the task_contract domain module with the four new types (TaskContractDocument, ContractedEntryRef, PreReviewGateViolation, PreReviewGateOutcome) and their inherent impls and derive trait impls. It also updates .harness/capabilities/impl-planner.md to declare task-contract.json as the third Phase 3 output artifact alongside impl-plan.json and task-coverage.json (IN-02).

T002 adds the pre_review_gate usecase module with the two secondary-port traits (TaskContractReaderPort, ImplCatalogSignalReaderPort), the primary service port (PreReviewGateService), the command and error types, and the PreReviewGateInteractor implementation. The interactor implements the three-phase gate logic: scope discovery, orphan detection, referential integrity check, and impl_catalog signal JOIN.

OQ-1 resolution (scope-relevant catalogue entries): task-contract checking is layer-scoped but can run in per-layer mode (Some(layer)) or all-layers mode (None). T008 invokes all-layers mode before review fix-local, so the 6 canonical TDDD layer ids (domain, usecase, infrastructure, cli_driver, cli, cli_composition) are visited in sequence; readable signal documents are checked and positively absent signal documents are treated as no entries to verify. Scope-relevant entries are the catalogue entries present in each loaded TypeSignalsDocument; task-contract.json ContractedEntryRef records are filtered where entry.layer equals that selected layer and compared against that full signal-derived set. Rationale: this uses the existing per-layer signal artifact as the authoritative scope catalogue, requires no filesystem probing or extra per-track declarations, and lets non-layer review scopes such as plan-artifacts reuse the same all-layers gate without inventing a signal document for the non-layer scope.
T001 must precede T002; both tasks are implemented in the same Rust feature batch (no commit between them).

sections.title: Infrastructure: codec and filesystem adapters
description: T003 adds task_contract_codec (TaskContractDocumentDto, TaskContractCodecError, decode, encode), task_contract_reader (FsTaskContractReader implementing TaskContractReaderPort), and impl_catalog_signal_reader (FsImplCatalogSignalReader implementing ImplCatalogSignalReaderPort) to libs/infrastructure.

FsImplCatalogSignalReader read_signals reads <items_dir>/<track_id>/<layer>-type-signals.json as domain::TypeSignalsDocument and fails closed on absence/read/decode errors; read_optional_signals returns Ok(None) only for positively classified absence so all-layers mode can treat missing signal documents as no entries to verify. No changes to SignalEvaluatorV2 or existing signal infrastructure (CN-05, OS-06); the adapter is a thin reader over the existing on-disk artifact produced by the TDDD signal calculation pipeline.
T003 depends on T001 (domain types) and T002 (port traits). It is part of the same Rust feature batch.

sections.title: CLI stack: driver, subcommand, and composition root
description: T004 adds TaskContractInput and TaskContractDriver in apps/cli-driver. The driver parses CLI strings into domain value objects and dispatches to PreReviewGateService::check, rendering PreReviewGateOutcome as CommandOutcome.

T006 adds TaskContractCompositionRoot in apps/cli-composition, which wires FsTaskContractReader + FsImplCatalogSignalReader + PreReviewGateInteractor + TaskContractDriver into one sotp task-contract check execution path. The composition root uses the CLI-provided items_dir to build the service, then dispatches TaskContractInput::Check through TaskContractDriver.

T005 adds clap-annotated TaskContractCommand and TaskContractCheckArgs in apps/cli, the two execute free functions, and registers `task-contract` as a top-level sotp subcommand. The items_dir flag defaults to 'track/items', matching the workspace convention for all track-reading commands.

T004 depends on T002 (service port); T006 depends on T003 and T004; T005 depends on T004 and T006 because execute_task_contract_check constructs TaskContractCompositionRoot and calls task_contract_check. All three tasks are implemented in the same Rust feature batch, with S3 implementation order T004 -> T006 -> T005.

sections.title: Tests: gate logic unit test coverage (AC-07)
description: T007 adds #[cfg(test)] unit tests inside libs/usecase/src/pre_review_gate.rs covering the five AC-07 scenarios with mock port implementations: (a) missing task-contract → Blocked/MissingTaskContract, (b) signal-document entry absent from task attribution → Blocked/OrphanEntry, (c) referential integrity violation → Blocked/InvalidEntryRef, (d) non-blue signal → Blocked/NonBlueSignal, (e) all blue + attribution complete → Passed with D5-compliant conformance_summary.
T007 depends on T002 (interactor under test) and T003 (concrete TypeSignalsDocument type used in mock responses). It is the final task in the Rust feature batch (T001-T007 committed together).

sections.title: Workflow wiring: Makefile.toml pre-review gate (separate commit)
description: T008 modifies [tasks.track-local-review-fix-codex] in Makefile.toml to run `bin/sotp task-contract check --track-id "$_TRACK_ID" --items-dir track/items` before `bin/sotp review fix-local "$@"`. The thin-chain shell script extracts --track-id from the forwarded $@ arguments, omits --layer so the use case enters all-layers mode, and then chains CODEX_BIN resolution plus review fix-local only if the gate succeeds. Gate failure from violations or signal read/decode errors halts the script before review fix-local is reached, satisfying AC-06 fail-closed semantics for loaded signal documents; positively absent signal documents are classified as no entries to verify by the optional reader.
No sibling track-local-review-fix-claude task exists; only track-local-review-fix-codex requires modification.
T008 is committed as a separate batch from the Rust feature batch (T001-T007).
